demo_headline: As documented, duration of the ibuprofen trial is missing — deniable today. The room said otherwise.

criteria.label: Symptom duration ≥ 6 weeks
criteria.summary: Chronic course dating to a college lifting injury; note documents multi-year history.

evidence.quoted_span: dating to a lifting episode in college and clearly aggravated by prolonged sitting
It's the same low back thing I've had for a few years.

criteria.label: Failed / incomplete conservative care ≥ 6 weeks
criteria.summary: Walking and hot showers spoken in room (not fully in note); note collapses modality to 'heat' and omits that the helpful routine lapsed months ago.

evidence.quoted_span: Walking. Hot showers. I had ibuprofen for a while, which worked, but the bottle ran out months ago and I never dealt with it.
evidence.supports: Conservative modalities + duration signal (months without refill)

evidence.quoted_span: It improves with walking and heat.

artifact.title: Draft note addendum — conservative care detail
artifact.body: Addendum (draft): Patient reports conservative measures including walking and hot showers. He previously used ibuprofen with good effect for a while, but the bottle ran out months ago and he has not restarted therapy. Functional aggravation with prolonged sitting at work.
provenance_links: Walking. Hot showers. I had ibuprofen for a while, which worked, but the bottle ran out months ago and I never dealt with it.

criteria.label: NSAID / analgesic trial
criteria.summary: Prior ibuprofen trial with benefit and months-long lapse is in the transcript; note lacks duration tokens ('for a while', 'months ago'). Today's MedicationRequest does not prove a completed prior trial.

evidence.quoted_span: I had ibuprofen for a while, which worked, but the bottle ran out months ago and I never dealt with it.
He previously used ibuprofen with good effect but has none at home.
evidence.supports: Note confirms prior use/response but not duration
Today's prescription / chart med label — not proof of ≥6-week prior trial

artifact.title: Draft note addendum — NSAID trial duration
artifact.body: Addendum (draft): Patient reports a prior ibuprofen trial that was helpful; supply ran out months ago and he has not resumed. New ibuprofen 400 mg prescribed today does not substitute for documenting the historical trial duration.
provenance_links: I had ibuprofen for a while, which worked, but the bottle ran out months ago and I never dealt with it.

criteria.label: Red flags assessed / neurologic exam
criteria.summary: Negative red-flag review and non-focal lumbar exam are in the note.

evidence.quoted_span: He denies radicular symptoms, numbness, weakness, bowel or bladder dysfunction, night pain, fevers, and weight loss.
straight-leg raise negative bilaterally; lower-extremity strength, sensation, and reflexes intact

artifact.title: Flagged claim (review) — prior MRI mentioned
artifact.body: Extracted claim: 'Patient reports prior lumbar MRI last year.' — NOT found in transcript or note. Shown so clinician can dismiss a bad extraction.

criteria.label: Functional limitation
criteria.summary: Sitting intolerance and altered gait are spoken; payer often wants explicit work/ADL impairment language. Targeted question to clinch.

evidence.quoted_span: when a release is close it's ten hours in a chair, and by Friday I'm walking like a question mark.

artifact.title: One targeted question
artifact.body: For the PA packet: has low back pain limited work duties or ADLs for ≥6 weeks (e.g., cannot sit through a shift, reduced lifting, missed tasks)?

fhir_gap_callout.title: Encounter FHIR gap
fhir_gap_callout.detail: Visit Condition resources list employment, stress, and gingivitis — not chronic low back pain. LBP appears in longitudinal chart labels only. Conversation remains the richest evidence layer.

packet_draft.title: Draft prior authorization packet — Lumbar MRI
